text.title: spec-advanced-guidelines Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for spec-advanced-guidelines as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Persona defined in section 3 but never referenced in user stories or AC.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Orphan personas provide no filtering signal. If a persona is not referenced in at least one user story, either the persona is unnecessary or the user stories are too generic.

failure-mode.description: Agent writes NFR as "The system should handle high traffic" or "Response times should be acceptable".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs produce qualitative NFR language because they lack knowledge of the specific system's performance profile. Require the agent to propose a specific number and explicitly mark it [estimate] — then the human validates or corrects the target.

failure-mode.description: AC covers only the happy path for all FRs, with no error or edge cases.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents default to positive-case thinking. Explicitly require error scenarios for every FR that involves user input, external calls, or state transitions. Ask: "What happens when this fails?"
